description_long: This is a circular stone kiosk resembling a classical well, featuring a robust stone base and four ornate columns supporting a thick, round entablature. The structure is partially overgrown with lush green ivy, which climbs up one column and wraps around part of the base, adding a touch of nature to the otherwise architectural form. Suspended from the center is a wooden bucket attached to a rope, suggesting a functional water-drawing mechanism. The stonework is detailed with carved patterns, including a lion head motif on one side, and scattered stones rest at the base. The kiosk is open on all sides, allowing easy access to the interior, and the stone surfaces show signs of weathering and age. The overall appearance is reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman or neoclassical garden architecture, blending utility with decorative elements.
description: A circular stone kiosk with ornate columns, a thick round roof, and a central bucket, partially covered in ivy and detailed with classical carvings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open well, suspended bucket, and a lion head carving on the base, with ivy climbing one column.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the dense ivy growth covering one column and part of the base, with the bucket visible inside.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the open structure, showing the bucket from behind and the architectural details of the columns.
description_view_3: Side view with a clear look at the lion head carving, scattered stones at the base, and less visible ivy compared to the opposite side.
attribute_roof_design: Flat, circular stone roof with thick overhang and carved decorative elements.
attribute_architectural_style: Classical Greco-Roman with neoclassical influences, featuring ornate columns and decorative stonework.
